title: 16 I-140 Approvals on June 4, 2026 - North America Immigration Law Group
content:
- 16 I-140 Approvals on June 4, 2026

## 16 I-140 Approvals on June 4, 2026
The following success stories highlight 16 I-140 petition approvals on June 4, 2026. These approvals include 16 NIW cases. Client names have been omitted to protect confidentiality.
Each summary below includes the applicant’s category, field, country of birth, current residence, current and proposed role, degree, publication and citation record, supporting letters, service center, and premium processing strategy.

### #1: NIW in Molecular Genetics
Born in Brazil and residing in the United States, this applicant works as a Postdoctoral Fellow and proposes to remain in the same role. The NIW petition was approved in Molecular Genetics.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 8 publications and 30 citations at the time of filing.
No supporting letters submitted. The case was adjudicated at the Texas Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.
Notable: Approved without recommendation or testimonial letters.

### #2: NIW in Plant Biotechnology
A Pakistani-born applicant residing in the United States is currently a Ph.D. candidate and proposes to become a Research Scientist. The NIW petition was approved in Plant Biotechnology.
The applicant holds a master's degree and had 4 publications and 56 citations at the time of filing.
No supporting letters submitted. The case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.
Notable: Approved without recommendation or testimonial letters.

### #3: NIW in Translational Cancer Genomics
Born in France and residing in the United States, this applicant works as a Research Scholar and proposes to remain in the same role. The NIW petition was approved in Translational Cancer Genomics.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 37 publications and 1,441 citations at the time of filing.
No supporting letters submitted. The case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with upfront premium processing.
Notable: Approved without recommendation or testimonial letters.

### #4: NIW in Clinical Medicine
A Nepalese-born applicant residing in the United States works as a Core Faculty Member and proposes to remain in the same role. The NIW petition was approved in Clinical Medicine.
The applicant holds an M.B.B.S. degree and had 6 publications and 65 citations at the time of filing.
Supported by 2 recommendation letters and 3 testimonial letters, the case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.
Notable: Approved in a non-STEM field.

### #5: NIW in Electrical and Computer Engineering
Born in Taiwan and residing in the United States, this applicant is currently a Ph.D. student and proposes to become a Research Scientist in industry. The NIW petition was approved in Electrical and Computer Engineering.
The applicant holds a master's degree and had 19 publications and 1,331 citations at the time of filing.
No supporting letters submitted. The case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.
Notable: Approved without recommendation or testimonial letters.

### #6: NIW in Computer Science
Born in China and residing in the United States, this applicant is currently a Ph.D. student and proposes to become a Tenure-track Professor. The NIW petition was approved in Computer Science.
The applicant holds a master's degree and had 9 publications and 122 citations at the time of filing.
Supported by 1 recommendation letter and 1 testimonial letter, the case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with upfront premium processing.

### #7: NIW in Biomedical Engineering
A Turkish-born applicant residing in the United States works as a Biomedical Engineer in industry and proposes to become a Senior Scientist in industry. The NIW petition was approved in Biomedical Engineering.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 18 publications and 367 citations at the time of filing.
No recommendation letters were submitted, but 3 testimonial letters were included. The case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.

### #8: NIW in Materials Science and Mechanical Engineering
Born in Taiwan and residing in the United States, this applicant works as a Postdoctoral Research Fellow and proposes to remain in the same role. The NIW petition was approved in Materials Science and Mechanical Engineering.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 12 publications and 487 citations at the time of filing.
No supporting letters submitted. The case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.
Notable: Approved without recommendation or testimonial letters.

### #9: NIW in Infectious Disease Research
A Bangladeshi-born applicant residing in the United States is currently a Ph.D. student and proposes to become a Postdoctoral Fellow. The NIW petition was approved in Infectious Disease Research.
The applicant holds a master’s degree and had 16 publications and 85 citations at the time of filing.
Supported by 1 recommendation letter and 3 testimonial letters, the case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.

### #10: NIW in Semiconductor Photonics
Born in China and residing in the United States, this applicant works as a Postdoctoral Scholar and proposes to remain in the same role. The NIW petition was approved in Semiconductor Photonics.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 15 publications and 389 citations at the time of filing.
No supporting letters submitted. The case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with upfront premium processing.
Notable: Approved without recommendation or testimonial letters.

### #11: NIW in Neuroimmunology
A Russian-born applicant residing in the United States is currently an MD student and proposes to become a Resident Physician. The NIW petition was approved in Neuroimmunology.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 5 publications and 111 citations at the time of filing.
Supported by 2 recommendation letters with no testimonial letters submitted, the case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.

### #12: NIW in Machine Learning
Born in Lebanon and residing in the United States, this applicant works as a CERA Research Analyst and proposes to remain in the same role. The NIW petition was approved in Machine Learning.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 10 publications and 76 citations at the time of filing.
Supported by 2 recommendation letters with no testimonial letters submitted, the case was adjudicated at the Texas Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.

### #13: NIW in Electrical Engineering
Born in China and residing in the United States, this applicant works as a Senior Design Engineer in industry and proposes to remain in the same role. The NIW petition was approved in Electrical Engineering.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 13 publications and 133 citations at the time of filing.
Supported by 2 recommendation letters and 1 testimonial letter, the case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.

### #14: NIW in Biomaterials Science and Engineering
A Turkish-born applicant residing in Turkey works as a Postdoctoral Researcher and proposes to become a Research Engineer. The NIW petition was approved in Biomaterials Science and Engineering.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 35 publications and 1,062 citations at the time of filing.
No supporting letters submitted. The case was transferred from the Texas Service Center to the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.
Notable: Approved without recommendation or testimonial letters while the applicant was residing outside the United States.

### #15: NIW in Materials Science and Engineering
Born in Bangladesh and residing in the United States, this applicant is currently a Ph.D. student and proposes to become a Materials Scientist and Engineer. The NIW petition was approved in Materials Science and Engineering.
The applicant holds a master's degree and had 7 publications and 35 citations at the time of filing.
No supporting letters submitted. The case was adjudicated at the Nebraska Service Center with a premium processing upgrade.
Notable: Approved without recommendation or testimonial letters.

### #16: NIW in Mechanical Engineering
Born in Vietnam and residing in the Republic of Korea, this applicant works as a Postdoctoral Fellow and proposes to remain in the same role. The NIW petition was approved in Mechanical Engineering.
The applicant holds a Ph.D. and had 12 publications and 133 citations at the time of filing.
No supporting letters submitted. The case was adjudicated at the Texas Service Center without premium processing and was approved in 681 days.
Notable: Approved without recommendation or testimonial letters while the applicant was residing outside the United States.
The key to our success is the way in which we present supporting evidence and provide the highest quality petition letters. With over 64,000 I-140 EB-1 ( EB-1A Alien of Extraordinary Ability; EB-1B Outstanding Researcher or Professor), EB-2 NIW ( National Interest Waiver ) and O-1 approvals, our firm has acquired substantial information about USCIS decisions, which gives us significant advantage over firms that only handle a small number of cases.
Based on our close track of USCIS internal memoranda, AAO decisions, and judicial review decisions, we have unique insight into the USCIS adjudication trends. Not only do we apply this insight into our approaches to our clients' cases, but we also carefully review all RFEs (Requests for Evidence), NOIDs (Notices of Intent to Deny), approvals, and denials issued on our cases so that we can further increase our understanding of USCIS strategies and decision-making processes. With the insight, we are able to advise our clients on the best ways to proceed with their petitions.
While other petitioners and attorneys may still use templates to draft recommendation letters or petition letters, our clients' recommendation letters and petition letters are tailored to their individual credentials to best persuade a USCIS officer that our clients meet the requirements of the category they are applying under and therefore their petitions deserve to be approved. To provide the best EB-1 and EB-2 NIW services, our law firm only selects attorneys who have received their professional Juris Doctor degrees from the top law schools in the U.S. and who have garnered rigorous analytical skills through years of experience.
